title: Windows Security, Permissions, Encryption, and Recovery · Device Forensics
summary: A realistic windows security, permissions, encryption, and recovery ticket contains one plausible but causally wrong diagnosis or unsafe action. Find the first unsupported inference and repair the evidence chain. Planned artifact: Repair a changed failure in Windows endpoint security and recovery baseline and publish the reproduction, evidence, repair, and regression record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Configure standard and administrative accounts, UAC, Windows Hello, MFA-capable identity, lock, session, sign-in options, recovery, and accessible alternatives.
Reason about users, groups, ownership, inheritance, explicit allow and deny, effective NTFS and share access, elevation, and least privilege.
Plan BitLocker scope, TPM use, startup protection, recovery-key custody, escrow, suspension, hardware change, backup, verification, and disposition.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# comptia-a-plus cumulative fictional support evidence portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic text-contract review only. They never execute learner
# commands, inspect the host, touch devices or accounts, connect to networks, open equipment,
# handle malware, erase media, contact people, or claim certification.
workspace: support
ticket-id: fictional-ticket
user-impact: [replace with user outcome, scope, severity, and urgency]
environment: [replace with asset, version, configuration, owner, dependency, and evidence time]
safety-authority: [replace with consent, hazards, data custody, privacy, access, and stop rules]
baseline: [replace with expected behavior, known-good state, conditions, and measurement]
symptom: [replace with observed behavior, reproduction, frequency, timing, and evidence]
recent-change: [replace with timeline, relevant changes, counterevidence, and unknowns]
hypothesis: [replace with falsifiable cause, competing explanation, prediction, and confidence]
test: [replace with one safe reversible discriminating test, changed case, and stop rule]
observation: [replace with actual result, provenance, rejected inference, and next decision]
repair-rollback: [replace with least-change repair, authority, backup, trigger, and restored state]
verify: [replace with user outcome, original, changed, side-effect, restart, and recurrence checks]
prevention: [replace with prevention, monitoring, owner, review date, and residual risk]
escalation: [replace with stop threshold, destination, evidence packet, and requested decision]
documentation: [replace with plain structured status, redaction, access, handoff, and uncertainty]
transfer-boundary: authorized disposable equipment or simulator only
review:

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Windows Account Security
steps.instruction:
debug case — Sensitive Windows data has inherited share permissions, a missing recovery key, weakened protection, and an inaccessible sign-in fallback. The debug variation 4 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Configure standard and administrative accounts, UAC, Windows Hello, MFA-capable identity, lock, session, sign-in options, recovery, and accessible alternatives.

Inspect the Windows Account Security trace for this debug case. Which record connects a precondition, observed state change, and boundary result?

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Windows Account Security.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for configure standard and administrative accounts, uac, windows hello, mfa-capable identity, lock, session, sign-in options, recovery, and accessible alternatives. For CompTIA A+ support Inspect the failure record · Debug Windows Account Security, keep Windows Account Security connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.text: For Windows Account Security, distinguish a claim from evidence by locating the relevant precondition, trace or invariant, and changed input result.

At CompTIA A+ support Implement the cause-level repair · Debug Windows Security Verify, trace this concrete input and predict user impact, safety and authority, baseline, reproduction, timeline, hypothesis, bounded test, observation, rejection, repair, rollback, original and changed-case verification, prevention, escalation, and controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: Sensitive Windows data has inherited share permissions, a missing recovery key, weakened protection, and an inaccessible sign-in fallback. The debug variation 6 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify identity, least privilege, encryption, recovery, patch, protection, firewall, sharing, remote access, audit, accessibility, and changed-user behavior.

steps.title: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Windows Security Verify
steps.instruction:
Write the debug evidence note for Windows Security Verify: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — Sensitive Windows data has inherited share permissions, a missing recovery key, weakened protection, and an inaccessible sign-in fallback. The debug variation 2 changes the user outcome, device, version, hazard, recent change, symptom, access need, competing cause, and transfer limit. Competency probe: Verify identity, least privilege, encryption, recovery, patch, protection, firewall, sharing, remote access, audit, accessibility, and changed-user behavior.

Evidence target: CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Windows Security Verify.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For CompTIA A+ support Publish the incident record · Debug Windows Security Verify, keep Windows Security Verify connected to mechanically reviewable user-impact, asset, safety, baseline, symptom, hypothesis, test, observation, repair, rollback, verification, escalation, documentation, and transfer decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Verify identity, least privilege, encryption, recovery, patch, protection, firewall, sharing, remote access, audit, accessibility, and changed-user behavior.
